commit.message:
fix: resolve zodResolver type mismatch in TrackerForm

Remove .default([]) from the tags field in formSchema. The .default([])
caused a split between the schema's input type (string[] | undefined) and
output type (string[]), making zodResolver produce a Resolver typed to the
input while useForm<FormValues> expected the output type — a type error at
compile time.

All defaultValues paths already initialize tags: [], so the default is
unnecessary.

feat: add exclude_paths support to organize_runtime_structure (#20)

Allows packages to exclude specific subdirectory trees from the
built package.  Each entry in exclude_paths is matched against
directory names and relative paths at any depth.

Use UniTask's External/ subtree as the first consumer: it contains
optional TMP, DOTween and Addressables extensions that require
3rd-party assemblies not guaranteed to be present in consumer
projects.

- UnityGenerator.organize_runtime_structure() now accepts an optional
  exclude_paths: list[str] parameter
- UnityGenerator._build_exclude_ignore() helper builds a shutil-
  compatible ignore callable from the list
- package_builder._build_git_package() reads exclude_paths from the
  package config dict and forwards it
- config/packages.yaml: unitask entry adds exclude_paths: ["External"]
- Three new tests cover exclude with/without existing Runtime folder
  and nested path exclusion

NavigationLink(value:) inside a custom LazyVGrid/ScrollView does NOT
cross the NavigationSplitView column boundary on iPad. It only works
inside a List where the split view can intercept the value. Tapping
cards in the sidebar silently did nothing on iPad.

Fix — MasterView:
- Added onSelect: (ToolItem) -> Void closure parameter
- ToolCard now uses Button { onSelect(item) } instead of
  NavigationLink(value: item) — navigation mechanism is fully
  delegated to ContentView

Fix — ContentView:
iPhone (.compact):
  @State navPath: NavigationPath
  onSelect closure appends the item to navPath
  NavigationStack(path: navPath) resolves navigationDestination
  in the same context — standard push navigation

iPad (.regular / Mac Catalyst):
  @State selectedItem: ToolItem?
  onSelect closure sets selectedItem directly
  NavigationSplitView detail column renders toolDetailView(for:)
  immediately when selectedItem changes — no cross-column linking
  needed at all

Both paths: press animation preserved via PressableCardStyle.
0 warnings, 0 errors.

commit.message:
fix: inject API_BASE_URL at runtime instead of build time (#86)

## Problem

The mobile app was hitting `mobile.alumap.tst.escalopa.com/auth/login`
instead of the backend. The root cause: `API_BASE_URL` was empty at
Docker build time, so `String.fromEnvironment` returned `""`, Dio used
an empty `baseUrl`, and the browser resolved `/auth/login` relative to
the current page origin → nginx 404.

## Solution

Switch from build-time baking to runtime injection so the API URL does
not need to be in the Docker image.

### How it works
1. `web/index.html` has a `<script>var apiBaseUrl =
"__API_BASE_URL__";</script>` placeholder.
2. `entrypoint.sh` (new nginx container entrypoint) replaces
`__API_BASE_URL__` with `$API_BASE_URL` env var before nginx starts.
3. `docker-stack.yml` sets `API_BASE_URL=https://api.${DOMAIN}` —
`DOMAIN` is already in `DEPLOY_DIR/.env` (written by Ansible), so no
secrets changes needed.
4. Flutter reads `window.apiBaseUrl` at runtime via `dart:js_interop`
(`api_config_web.dart`), falling back to `String.fromEnvironment` for
native builds.

## Changes
- `web/index.html` — runtime config placeholder
- `lib/data/config/api_config{.dart,_stub.dart,_web.dart}` —
platform-conditional URL resolver
- `lib/app.dart`, `lib/data/secrets/secrets_manager.dart` — use
`getApiBaseUrl()`
- `entrypoint.sh` — replaces placeholder at container start
- `Dockerfile` — copies entrypoint, switches `CMD` → `ENTRYPOINT`
- `docker-stack.yml` — passes `API_BASE_URL=https://api.${DOMAIN}` at
deploy time

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(hardening): round 13 — W28 W31 W32 W33 W34 fixed with TDD

W28: handlePrune gains checkDaemonRunning injection — refuses to prune while a daemon
     is running (cross-process TOCTOU: daemon may write updated manifest after prune reads
     but before prune writes, causing ghost sessions to reappear).

W31: handlePrune now exhaustively matches ManifestLoadError — IoError and CorruptData
     return false (not true). Old Error _ catch-all treated a corrupt/unreadable manifest
     identically to NotFound and silently reported 'nothing to prune'.

W32: Startup binary manifest load now uses exhaustive match with correct severity:
     NotFound → LogInformation + succeedSpan (expected first-run condition),
     IoError  → LogWarning + failSpan (file exists but unreadable),
     CorruptData → LogError + failSpan (permanent corruption, history lost).
     Old code used LogInformation + succeedSpan for all three cases.

W33: testCycleTimer shutdown: timeout increased 1s→3s; Dispose is now conditional on
     Wait() returning true. If Wait times out, the undisposed MRSE prevents
     ObjectDisposedException when the timer system tries to signal the WaitHandle
     after Dispose.

W34: mergeManifestWithExisting return type changed from Result<_, string> to
     Result<_, ManifestLoadError>. Callers updated with exhaustive OR-pattern match
     (IoError|CorruptData for error string extraction; NotFound arm for completeness).
     Typed errors let callers distinguish transient IoError (retriable) from
     permanent CorruptData (needs manual recovery).

handlePrune signature refactored: (dir, log, checkDaemonRunning, flags) for testability
and DI instead of (infra, flags). Callsite passes DaemonState.SageFsDir/infra.Log/
DaemonState.read. Tests inject NullLogger + noDaemon/fakeDaemon closures.

fix: preserve upstream asmdef versionDefines; add missing package dependencies (#19)

* fix: serve tarballs from GitHub Pages to avoid auth requirement

UPM cannot apply .upmconfig.toml auth to tarball URLs on a different
host than the configured scopedRegistry. GitHub Packages tarballs
always require auth even for public packages, causing 401 errors.

The fix: save the tarball bytes to dist/registry/ alongside the
packument so that GitHub Pages (a public static host) serves both.
UPM resolves packuments and downloads tarballs without any auth.

Changes:
- pages_publisher: accept tarball_data + pages_base_url params;
  write tarball to registry_dir and use Pages URL in packument
- package_publisher: pass tarball_data + PAGES_BASE_URL env to
  PagesPublisher; remove _fetch_github_tarball_url (no longer needed)
- workflow: restore dist/registry from cache before publish (preserves
  tarballs from prior runs); save cache after; set PAGES_BASE_URL env
- tests: replace fetch-tarball-url tests with tarball_data/pages_base_url
  forwarding tests; add TestPagesPublisherTarballStorage class (4 tests)

* fix(cli): runtime ESM patch for npx on Node 24+ (#265)

When npx caches @bradygaster/squad-cli, it skips postinstall scripts,
so the install-time ESM patch never runs. This adds a runtime
Module._resolveFilename intercept that rewrites 'vscode-jsonrpc/node'
to 'vscode-jsonrpc/node.js' before Node's module system tries to
resolve it. Works on both Node 22 and 24+.

Closes #265

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
